description: This file controls a skill's tooltip description and how it is displayed on the Skill Tree. Any column field name starting with "*" is considered a comment field and is not used by the game.
source: Colonnes du .txt (BKVince 3.2) ; types/descriptions du guide TXT eezstreet-d2rdoc.

columns.name: skilldesc
columns.description: The name of the skill description, as a reference for associated Data files

columns.name: SkillPage
columns.description: Determines which page on the Skill tree to display the skill

columns.name: SkillRow
columns.description: Determines which row on the Skill tree page to display the skill

columns.name: SkillColumn
columns.description: Determines which column on the Skill tree page to display the skill

columns.name: ListRow
columns.description: Determines which row the skill will be listed in, for the skill select UI.

columns.name: IconCel
columns.description: Determines the icon asset for displaying the skill. This requires an ID value based on the skillicon files. Class specific skills use their designated class skillicon files (controlled by the skills.charclass), and non-class skills use the global skillicon file. This will use the value as the standard icon to display, and the next value (value + 1) as the button pressed icon display.

columns.name: HireableIconCel
columns.description: Determines the icon asset for displaying the skill on a hireable (mercenary). This requires an ID value based on the hireable skillicon files. This will use the value as the standard icon to display, and the next value (value + 1) as the button pressed icon display.

columns.name: str name
columns.description: Uses a string to display as the skill name

columns.name: str short
columns.description: Uses a string to display as the skill description in shortcuts or when selecting a skill

columns.name: str long
columns.description: Uses a string to display as the skill description on the Skill Tree

columns.name: str alt
columns.description: Uses a string to display the skill name on the Character Screen when the skill is selected

columns.name: descdam
columns.description: Use a function to calculate a skill's damage and determine how to display it. These functions sometimes require certain skill fields, especially the damage related fields.

columns.name: ddam calc1
columns.description: Integer calc value used as a possible parameter for the .descdam function Skill scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: ddam calc2
columns.description: Integer calc value used as a possible parameter for the .descdam function Skill scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: p1dmelem
columns.description: Used for skills that have charge-ups to display the damage on the Character Screen, controls the elemental type for that charge

columns.name: p1dmmin
columns.description: Used for skills that have charge-ups to display the damage on the Character Screen, controls the minimum damage for that charge Skill scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: p1dmmax
columns.description: Used for skills that have charge-ups to display the damage on the Character Screen, controls the maximum damage for that charge Skill scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: p2dmelem
columns.description: Used for skills that have charge-ups to display the damage on the Character Screen, controls the elemental type for that charge

columns.name: p2dmmin
columns.description: Used for skills that have charge-ups to display the damage on the Character Screen, controls the minimum damage for that charge Skill scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: p2dmmax
columns.description: Used for skills that have charge-ups to display the damage on the Character Screen, controls the maximum damage for that charge Skill scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: p3dmelem
columns.description: Used for skills that have charge-ups to display the damage on the Character Screen, controls the elemental type for that charge

columns.name: p3dmmin
columns.description: Used for skills that have charge-ups to display the damage on the Character Screen, controls the minimum damage for that charge Skill scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: p3dmmax
columns.description: Used for skills that have charge-ups to display the damage on the Character Screen, controls the maximum damage for that charge Skill scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: descatt
columns.description: Used to display the overall Attack Rating of the skill in the Character Screen

columns.name: descmissile1
columns.description: Links a missile to be used as a reference value for calculations

columns.name: descmissile2
columns.description: Links a missile to be used as a reference value for calculations

columns.name: descmissile3
columns.description: Links a missile to be used as a reference value for calculations

columns.name: descline1
columns.description: Uses an ID value to select a description function to format the string value. Displays this text as the current level and next level description lines in the skill tooltip.

columns.name: desctexta1
columns.description: String value used as the first possible string parameter for the .descline# function

columns.name: desctextb1
columns.description: String value used as the second possible string parameter for the .descline# function

columns.name: desccalca1
columns.description: Integer calculation value used as the first possible numeric parameter for the .descline# function Skill scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: desccalcb1
columns.description: Integer calculation value used as the second possible numeric parameter for the .descline# function Skill scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: item proc text
columns.description: String value used as an override format for when the skill appears as a "chance to cast" property on an item. Can be formatted to include .descline# in the string using "%s" entries. Leave blank to ignore and use the string format in itemstatcost.

columns.name: item proc descline count
columns.description: Integer value for how many .descline# entries should be formatted into the .item proc text string.
